scope_note: Caltech’s official first-year application pages do not publish a minimum GPA cutoff or admitted-student GPA distribution. This panel therefore summarizes the transcript materials Caltech actually requires.
policy_summary: Caltech does not publish a minimum GPA. Instead, it requires official secondary-school transcripts, a school report, and a mid-year report when senior grades become available.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Official transcripts from secondary school grades 9-11 must be submitted by a school official.
Caltech also requires a school report from the high-school counselor and a mid-year report once first-term senior grades are available.
